name: Basic Cornmeal Biscuit
recipeIngredient: 4 tablespoons Butter, cut into small pieces
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 425°F. In a food processor or large bowl, mix 1 1/2 cups of the cornmeal with the flour, salt, sugar, cayenne pepper, baking powder, and baking soda using a fork until well combined. Add the butter pieces and work them into the mixture using your fingertips until the dough resembles coarse cornmeal.
In a separate bowl, gently combine the egg and buttermilk. Stir in all but 2 tablespoons of this liquid mixture into the dry ingredients until a uniformly moistened dough forms. Transfer half of the dough to a lightly floured surface and knead briefly if needed.
Shape the remaining dough into an even circle about 1/2 inch thick, then cut with a 2-inch or 3-inch biscuit cutter. Place the biscuits close together on a greased cookie sheet. Brush the tops with the reserved buttermilk egg mixture.
Bake in the preheated oven until puffed and golden brown, about 12 minutes. Allow to cool for a few minutes before serving. Yields about 1 dozen biscuits.
